project.title: Targeted protein editing with an antibody-based system
project.projectDescription: The chemical modification of proteins is one of the major mechanisms used to regulate the properties and functions of these macromolecules in the cell. It is therefore of great interest to develop tools to exploit this type of modifications for applications in molecular biology, medicine and biotechnology. Here we present a method of using antibodies to perform post-translational covalent modifications of endogenous proteins in complex environments by exploiting proximity-driven chemistry. The method is based on the ability of antibodies to hold a weakly reactive group close to its intended site of reaction by binding the target protein on a nearby epitope. We characterise this approach by modifying the green fluorescent protein in increasingly complex environments, and illustrate its applicability by targeting the disease-associated protein beta-2 microglobulin. This dataset contains proteomics data for the identification of the modification site(s) on PEABS targets.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Purified GFP (27701.31 Da) or beta-2 microglobulin (b2m, 11860.34 Da) was incubated with PEABS - a chemically reactive antibody capable of transferring a rhodamine B or biotin group to a primary amine of its target. After 4-6 hours, intact LC-MS analysis revealed predominantly single-modified target protein population. The components were then separated by SDS-PAGE and the gel band(s) corresponding to GFP or b2m were excised. The excised gel bands were subjected to chymotrypsin digestion followed by LC-MS/MS analysis at the Cambridge Centre for Proteomics. A 60-minute standard LC-MS/MS gradient was employed.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Spectra were searched against the common Repository of Adventitious Proteins (cRAP), containing e.g., human keratins, bovine serum albumin, porcine trypsin, and the target protein of interest using the Mascot software. Up to two missed cleavages were allowed, and the precursor mass tolerance was set to 20 ppm and the fragment to 0.1 Da. Carbamidomethylation at cysteine was defined as a fixed modification, and three variable modifications were defined: deamidation at asparagine or glutamine; oxidation at methionine; and acylation with biotin or rhodamine, respectively, at lysine and the N-terminus.
